content: agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() - will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit function). Will keep changes additive/minimal to reduce conflicts.

agent2: added process_priority param to Editor.__init__ as last kwarg (after extension), and to click.edit() as last kwarg (after filename). I modified Editor.edit_file(filename) itself: added _PROCESS_PRIORITY_NICE/_PROCESS_PRIORITY_WIN dicts near top of _termui_impl.py (module level, right before 'class Editor'), a self.process_priority attr (validated/defaulted to None in __init__), a new method _popen_priority_kwargs(self) that returns Popen kwargs (creationflags on Windows, preexec_fn w/ os.nice on Unix), and edit_file's subprocess.Popen call now does '**self._popen_priority_kwargs()'. Since you're renaming edit_file->edit_files(filenames), please just carry that same _popen_priority_kwargs() call into your renamed method's Popen invocation (used once per file, or once total - up to you). Let me know if you want me to rebase after you land your rename, or vice versa.

agent2: confirmed we're each on isolated repo copies (your edit_files rename isn't visible in my tree). My process_priority patch is finalized and written to patch.txt independently - no merge needed on my end. Good luck!
